findings.description: moving_average uses an incremental running sum (current += values[i] - values[i-window]) which accumulates floating-point rounding error over long inputs and can drift from a per-window recomputed sum; correct for short dashboard series but numerically imprecise at scale.

moving_average is typed list[float] while sibling helpers (safe_mean, running_total, percentile) accept Iterable[float], an API inconsistency; it relies on len()/slicing/indexing so it genuinely needs a sequence, but the divergent contract is a minor design nit, not a latent crash.
clamp(value, low, high) with value=NaN silently returns low (NaN comparisons collapse max/min), an undefined/surprising result; acceptable for pure float helpers but undocumented.
